summary: Israel completed a large‑scale “Sulfur and Fire” drill on its Jordanian border and intercepted a Hezbollah rocket, while a precision strike in Gaza killed Hamas military chief Izz al‑Din al‑Haddad. The United States is reportedly preparing a new operation against Iran, and the UK has dispatched Typhoon fighters to Qatar. Iran faces a daily gasoline shortfall of at least 20 million litres, heightening domestic pressure.
key_points: IDF’s “Sulfur and Fire” drill tested rapid deployment of 96th and 80th divisions on the Jordanian border.
Hezbollah rocket targeting Israeli troops in southern Lebanon was intercepted.
Israeli airstrike in Gaza’s Rimal district killed Hamas military chief Izz al‑Din al‑Haddad.
US officials are planning a possible new operation against Iran, including special‑forces uranium extraction.
UK announced deployment of four Typhoon fighters to Qatar to bolster regional air presence.
Iran reports a daily gasoline deficit of 20 million L, with production capped at 115 million L.
US‑Lebanon cease‑fire extended by 45 days, easing immediate border tensions.

sections.executive_summary: Israel’s extensive “Sulfur and Fire” drill on the Jordanian frontier demonstrated rapid mobilization of reserve forces and multi‑domain coordination, underscoring heightened readiness after the Oct 7 lessons. Simultaneously, a precision strike in Gaza eliminated Hamas military chief Izz al‑Din al‑Haddad, marking a significant degradation of Hamas command. The United States is reportedly finalising a new operation against Iran, while the UK has moved four Typhoon fighters to Qatar, indicating expanding external involvement in the theater.

points: Iran’s Energy Committee disclosed a daily gasoline shortfall of at least 20 million litres, with production limited to 110‑115 million L against a consumption of 130‑135 million L, raising risk of civil unrest (Source: @VahidOnline).

Former US officials claim the US is preparing a new operation against Iran, possibly involving special‑forces extraction of high‑enriched uranium, requiring thousands of support personnel (Source: @kianmeli1).
Iranian officials warned that U.S. sanctions and a U.S.‑led naval blockade could soon halt oil pumping, exacerbating the fuel crisis (Source: @VahidOnline).
Iran’s senior military leadership in Kurdistan announced a final push to exploit perceived enemy weakness, hinting at possible escalatory actions (Source: @kianmeli1).

The IDF completed the “Sulfur and Fire” drill on the Jordanian border, involving the 96th and 80th divisions, special forces, fighter jets and helicopters, with 100 % reserve mobilization in the 96th Division (Source: @manniefabian/58251).
Hezbollah fired a rocket at Israeli troops in southern Lebanon; the Israeli air defence intercepted it minutes later (Source: @manniefabian/58250).

An Israeli airstrike in Gaza’s Rimal neighbourhood hit a residential building and killed Hamas military chief Izz al‑Din al‑Haddad, confirmed by multiple sources (Source: @manniefabian/58256, @Faytuks_Network/2736).
Home Front Command reported multiple Red Alerts along the northern confrontation line (Metulla) and at Sdeh Meron, but all incidents were resolved without casualties (Source: @tzevaadom_en).
US‑Lebanon cease‑fire was extended by 45 days, reducing immediate border pressure (Source: @asafroz/49236).
Israeli strike in Gaza’s Rimal neighbourhood killed Izz al‑Din al‑Haddad, a senior Hamas military figure, and damaged a residential building (Source: @manniefabian/58256).
Yedioth reports a targeted car bomb in the Al‑Wahda neighbourhood of Gaza, resulting in three fatalities near Al‑Yarmouk stadium (Source: @yediotnews25/129327).
Another report from Yedioth notes a successful strike on a vehicle in the Rimal district, confirming ongoing precision operations (Source: @yediotnews25/129324).
Palestinian media announced the funeral of Hamas leader Izz al‑Din al‑Haddad, indicating potential rallying effects among militants (Source: @manniefabian/58262).
Hezbollah launched a rocket at Israeli forces in southern Lebanon; Israeli air defence intercepted it, preventing casualties (Source: @manniefabian/58250).
US‑Lebanon cease‑fire extension by 45 days aims to stabilize the northern front (Source: @asafroz/49236).
No further large‑scale hostilities reported from Lebanon in the latest feed, suggesting a temporary lull.
No direct operational reports from Syria or Iraq appear in the provided messages; intelligence focus remains on Iran, Israel, Gaza, and Lebanon.
UK announced deployment of four Typhoon fighters to Qatar to reinforce regional air defence amid rising tensions (Source: @kianmeli1/87428).
US‑UK discussions on increased security funding in Britain (£18 bn) reflect broader Western concern over Middle‑East instability (Source: @asafroz/49242).
Press TV commentary links US aggression toward Iran to increased Chinese leverage, indicating Gulf states may face shifting power dynamics (Source: @presstv/190083).

key_developments: Israel’s “Sulfur and Fire” drill on the Jordanian border mobilized 100 % of the 96th Division’s reservists and tested rapid air‑ground strikes, showing heightened readiness (Source: @manniefabian/58251).
Hezbollah’s rocket launch at Israeli troops in southern Lebanon was intercepted by Israeli air defence, preventing casualties (Source: @manniefabian/58250).
An Israeli airstrike in Gaza’s Rimal neighbourhood killed Hamas military chief Izz al‑Din al‑Haddad, a key architect of the Oct 7 attack (Source: @manniefabian/58256).
The United States is reportedly preparing a new operation against Iran, including possible special‑forces extraction of high‑enriched uranium (Source: @kianmeli1).
Iran reports a daily gasoline deficit of at least 20 million litres, with production unable to meet consumption, risking domestic unrest (Source: @VahidOnline).
The United Kingdom has dispatched four Typhoon fighter jets to Qatar to bolster regional air defence amid escalating tensions (Source: @kianmeli1/87428).
The US‑Lebanon cease‑fire was extended by 45 days, temporarily easing the northern front (Source: @asafroz/49236).
sections.threat_assessment: Israel is reinforcing its eastern and northern fronts through large‑scale drills and rapid interception capabilities, while simultaneously degrading Hamas command structures in Gaza. Iran faces internal pressure from a severe fuel shortage and external pressure from a potential US operation, raising the risk of broader escalation. Hezbollah’s limited rocket activity suggests a restrained but still active threat to Israel’s northern border.
sections.regional_response: Western allies are increasing military support, exemplified by the UK’s Typhoon deployment to Qatar and Britain’s announced £18 bn security boost, indicating a collective effort to contain the conflict. Iran’s internal fuel crisis may force the regime to adopt a more aggressive posture to divert attention, while the US appears to be calibrating a new covert operation against Iranian nuclear assets. Regional actors such as Lebanon and Qatar are positioning themselves as buffers, seeking to limit spill‑over.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of Iranian fuel logistics is critical, as shortages could trigger civil unrest or prompt the regime to leverage proxy forces. The success of the Israeli strike on al‑Haddad may provoke retaliatory attacks from Hamas, requiring heightened alert in Gaza‑adjacent Israeli districts. The US’s contemplated operation against Iran, if executed, could involve high‑risk special‑forces missions, demanding close watch on intelligence sharing and regional troop movements.
